title: Habitat: a thin institutional-knowledge overlay over related repos
content:
A 'habitat' is a named group of related repos that share authored institutional knowledge — the domain rules plus a map of how the repos relate — so an agent working in any member repo already knows them. Mechanically tiny: a directory that symlinks its member repos + carries ONE authored CLAUDE.md (rules + short member map). Loaded when working in a member repo (or from the habitat dir). It is a VIEW OVER the repos, not a store that copies them. This is the professional first step of a larger cross-host/cross-domain endeavor.

Framing (Ryan, 2026-07-15): the through-line is cross-repo COORDINATION + institutional-KNOWLEDGE development — an agent in a habitat both knows the domain rules and knows how to act across the member repos.

title: Should the habitat CLAUDE.md knowledge be version-controlled?
content: Yes — version-controlled. Decided 2026-07-15 (habituate-10): each habitat is its own git repo, so the authored CLAUDE.md/README + member symlinks are tracked in the habitat's repo, while habituate keeps habitats/ gitignored. Knowledge is durable in the habitat repo; seeds remain the deliberation record.

title: Each habitat is its own git repo
content: Decided 2026-07-15: every habitat under habituate/habitats/<name>/ is its OWN git repo (git init + committed CLAUDE.md/README + member symlinks). This version-controls the institutional knowledge while habituate keeps its habitats/ folder gitignored — habituate tracks the tool + deliberation; each habitat tracks its own knowledge. Resolves the open question habituate-1.1.

title: Ground rules: how a habitat is used in practice
content: How Habituate is meant to be used day-to-day (Ryan, 2026-07-15): you put your working directory INTO a habitat and fire up agents there for multi-repo work; the agent operates across the symlinked member repos — reading/writing code, seeds, and beads in each. The habitat's CLAUDE.md gives the agent the map + routing so it knows which repo owns what. Children capture the specific rules.

title: Habitat is a workspace: cd in, fire up agents for multi-repo work
content: The habitat directory is a WORKSPACE. Ryan cd's into habituate/habitats/<name>/ and starts agents from there for multi-repo work. Because the habitat's own CLAUDE.md IS the cwd's CLAUDE.md, it auto-loads — the agent gets the map + rules + routing immediately. (The per-member CLAUDE.local.md wiring covers the OTHER entry point: starting deep inside a single member repo.)

title: Agent routes work to the owning member repo (code, seeds, beads)
content: An agent in a habitat must know which member repo owns which kind of work and scatter code/seeds/beads into THAT repo. Vocabulary examples: 'new vocab / new file to load' => ohdsi_supplemental_vocabs (vocabs/*.tsv); 'generate new ICD-10 artifacts' => one_offs/icd10cm; 'build/deploy the latest vocabularies' => vocabulation; lookups/consumers => code_set_catalog / code_collector. This routing is AUTHORED into the habitat CLAUDE.md (promoted as a lodestone) so it loads automatically.

title: No habitat-level beads/seeds — scatter into member repos
content: A habitat does NOT get its own beads/seeds DB (2026-07-15). Seeds/beads land in the member repo that owns the work (per the routing rule). NB: habituate the PROJECT has its own seeds/beads for the tool's deliberation; individual habitats do not.

title: Will we ever need multi-repo orchestration via beads/seeds at the habitat level (cross-repo deps, or a habitat-level tracker)? Currently believed NO — revisit if coordination across member repos ever demands it.
Tooling: make habitat operations deterministic
content: Most habitat operations are mechanical and scriptable — candidate for deterministic tooling. Keep the AUTHORED knowledge hand-written (principle: authored, not synthesized); automate only the scaffolding. Children: a habituate CLI and a shell hop command.

title: Shell hop: h <habitat> -> cd + tmux session (create/attach)
content: Now a subcommand 'habituate hop <name>' backed by a shell wrapper installed via home-manager (Hammond): cd into habituate/habitats/<name>/ and create-or-attach a tmux session of the same name (e.g. 'habituate hop vocabulary'). The wrapper resolves the path via the habituate binary, then does the cd + tmux itself (a subprocess can't change the parent shell). CLI name is 'habituate' (not shortened to h).

title: A habituate CLI (create / add-member / wire / doctor)
content: Decided 2026-07-15: build it in BASH, named 'habituate' (full name, not shortened). Subcommands: create <name> [members...], doctor [name], hop <name>. create scaffolds a habitat (mkdir, symlink members at correct relative depth, write a CLAUDE.md skeleton with map/rules/routing stubs, wire each member's CLAUDE.local.md + .git/info/exclude, git init + commit). doctor verifies symlinks resolve, wiring intact, each member imports this habitat + is git-ignored, habitat is a git repo. hop needs shell integration (a subprocess cannot cd the parent) — delivered via home-manager (Hammond). Keep it thin/legible: no daemon, no state.

title: Permissions when creating a habitat (cross-repo agent access)
content: Investigated 2026-07-15 (headless test): an agent started IN the habitat dir CAN write to member repos through the habitat's symlinks — verified by writing a scratch file via ./icd10cm/... which landed in one_offs/icd10cm/. Because members are symlinked UNDER the habitat dir, their paths are nominally within cwd, so Claude Code's cwd-scoping is satisfied and NO additionalDirectories/--add-dir grant is needed. Caveats: default (non-acceptEdits) mode still prompts per-edit; each member repo's own .claude/settings.json allow/deny still applies; resolving a symlink to its real out-of-cwd path could re-trigger a prompt. Net: the symlink-into-habitat design gives cross-repo access essentially for free — no special permission setup at create time.

title: CLAUDE.md layering v2: general instructions in a symlinked HABITAT.md
content: Verified 2026-07-15: general habitat-agent instructions live in a TRACKED file habituate/HABITAT.md, symlinked as habitats/CLAUDE.md — confirmed to load for every habitat-dir session via walk-up (through the symlink; positive control passed). Keeps it version-controlled (target is tracked; the symlink under gitignored habitats/ is a local materialization) AND distinct from habituate/CLAUDE.md (which stays habituate-project / tool-dev instructions). Walk-up chain in a habitat: habitat/<name>/CLAUDE.md + habitats/CLAUDE.md(->HABITAT.md) + habituate/CLAUDE.md + ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. NB: habituate/CLAUDE.md STILL loads in a habitat (walk-up), so the split is by CONTENT — move habitat-behavior into HABITAT.md, leave tool-dev in habituate/CLAUDE.md.

title: Member repos stay habitat-agnostic; primary flow is agent-in-habitat-dir
content: Vision (Ryan, 2026-07-15): the primary flow is launching an agent IN the habitat directory; it reaches into member repos via the symlinks. Accessing a member repo DIRECTLY will be rare and is a deliberate, repo-specific choice — with NO expectation that the member repo understands the habitat(s) it belongs to (a member can belong to several habitats). Implications: (1) DROP the per-member CLAUDE.local.md wiring (revises habituate-8) — members stay agnostic; (2) the habitat CLAUDE.md need NOT be self-contained — it can rely on the symlinked parent. The habitat loads because the habitat dir is cwd, not because members import it.

title: Habitat symlinks: use absolute paths
content: Direction (Ryan, 2026-07-15): use ABSOLUTE paths for the member symlinks a habitat contains (and in habituate create). Rationale: habitats are local, gitignored, regenerable materializations, so the 'relative survives a whole-tree move' benefit doesn't really apply; absolute is simpler, readable, and removes depth/nesting math (e.g. one_offs/icd10cm needed ../../../). The small within-tree symlink habitats/CLAUDE.md -> ../HABITAT.md can stay relative. Revises the CLI (habituate-13.2).

title: Habitat creation is agent-driven; the CLI must be discoverable
content: Ryan does NOT intend to run 'habituate create' himself — an AGENT should know to use it (e.g. when asked 'make a habitat for X'). Implication: document the CLI (create/doctor/list/hop) where an agent will load it — in habituate/CLAUDE.md and/or HABITAT.md — so an agent working on habituate, or in a habitat, knows the command exists and how to use it. Ergonomics should be agent-robust: clear args, good errors, doctor to verify.

title: In a habitat, use seeds (not plan mode) for planning — in the owning member's seeds
content: Observed (Ryan, 2026-07-15): an agent in the vocabulary habitat used Claude Code PLAN MODE for planning instead of capturing it as seeds. Reinforce in HABITAT.md: for planning/deliberation, use SEEDS — specifically the existing seeds repo of the MEMBER project that owns the work (per routing, habituate-11.2); to do so, cd into that member (via its symlink in the habitat) and run seeds there. All 5 current members have seeds + beads initialized (verified 2026-07-15). Plan mode is fine for get-approval-before-acting but it is EPHEMERAL, not the durable record. Still NO seeds/beads at the HABITAT level yet (reaffirms habituate-11.3 / habituate-16).

title: Problem: we keep re-explaining institutional knowledge to a forgetful agent
content: Recurring example: working in ohdsi_supplemental_vocabs, the agent keeps assuming upstream vocabularies churn constantly and that WE must figure out how to reintroduce our customizations — not knowing vocabulation already handles exactly that. Third time we've had the same conversation. The knowledge exists (in our heads, scattered across repos) but isn't AT HAND for the agent when it works. A habitat puts it at hand.

title: 'Peripheral' member: accurate label, behavioral meaning still open
content: Decided 2026-07-15: keep code_set_catalog + code_collector as members of the vocabulary habitat but mark them PERIPHERAL — an accurate reflection (downstream consumers, developed in parallel). Ryan: only an hour into using habitats; NOT setting hard membership rules yet. What 'peripheral' means for AGENT BEHAVIOR toward such a member is UNDEFINED and deliberately not speculated on (rejected the treat-as-read-only guess). Note it; expect more membership nuance ahead; revisit as patterns emerge.

title: Cross-habitat overlap -> concurrent-agent collisions; a standard activity diagnostic
content:
A member can belong to several habitats (and Ryan works some in parallel), so two agents in different habitats can touch the same member repo and collide (race-condition-ish). Not solving coordination now — just DETECTION. Standard activity diagnostic (design; NOT built, per Ryan): per member repo, report in-flight-work signals — (1) dirty tree via git status --porcelain, (2) .git/index.lock present = active git op, (3) last-commit freshness via git log -1 --format=%cr, (4) uncommitted .beads/issues.jsonl or .seeds/seeds.jsonl = parallel task/deliberation capture (exactly what surfaced in code_set_catalog). Could later be 'habituate doctor --activity'. HABITAT.md now tells agents to watch for this before sweeping changes. Resolution (locking? branch-per-agent? advisory) deferred.

BUILT 2026-07-15 as 'habituate activity [<name>]' — deterministic; flags dirty tree / .git/index.lock / uncommitted .beads-.seeds; shellcheck-clean + tested. Actual race RESOLUTION (locking/coordination) still deferred.

title: HABITAT.md points agents to 'seeds prime' / 'bd prime'
content: Observed 2026-07-15: a live vocabulary-habitat agent struggled to understand seeds. Fix: HABITAT.md now tells agents new to the workflow to run 'seeds prime' (and 'bd prime') in the owning member repo — they print the AI-oriented workflow cheat sheet. Takes effect for NEW sessions; a running session should just be told to run 'seeds prime' now.

title: Habitat-level bead coordination: run beads across member repos in dependency order
content: Need (Ryan, 2026-07-15): from a habitat, execute/implement beads across MULTIPLE member repos IN THE PROPER ORDER — hopping between repos to get cross-repo ordering right. Motivating case (from the live vocabulary-habitat session): a tab-delimited -> .tsv rename in lockstep across 3 repos, seeded as ohdsi-supplemental-vocabs-18 (primary) with vocabulation-15 + icd10cm-4 depending on it. Could be a new skill, a habituate CLI command, or a wrapper around the existing per-repo bead flow. Ryan is open on mechanism. Children capture the sub-problems.

title: Existing bead skills are single-repo; run them per member, not from the habitat
content: From the vocabulary-habitat agent's session (2026-07-15): /bead-process (and /bead-go, /bead-setup) hard-assume ONE repo where current-dir = repo-holding-beads = repo-work-lands-in = one project type. A habitat has NO beads of its own, so STOP CHECK 2 (bd ready in cwd) finds nothing from the habitat dir. They DO work when run INSIDE each member repo. So per-repo draining works today; the gap is cross-repo SEQUENCING, not running the skill.

title: Cross-repo bead ordering: where does the sequence come from?
content: Decided (Ryan, 2026-07-15): cross-repo ordering lives in a HABITAT-LEVEL manifest, NOT as cross-repo bead deps. Reject bead-level cross-repo references: (1) bd can't resolve them (separate DBs); (2) they'd need an external coordinator regardless; (3) they VIOLATE member-agnosticism — a member's bead would encode knowledge of other repos / its habitat. A manifest at the habitat keeps the ordering where the coordinator lives and keeps members ignorant. Precedent: agents have used a manifest-style approach before.

title: Proposed shape: a habitat 'drain beads' wrapper
content:
One command/skill that drains beads across all member repos: (1) discover ready beads in every member (bd ready in each); (2) order them by cross-repo deps (see 23.2); (3) for each bead in order: hop to its OWNING member repo (per habitat routing), worktree that repo, dispatch the right specialist to implement, verify locally, merge/close THERE. The habitat stays a view; beads/worktree/git remain per-member-repo. Could be 'habituate <verb>' or a habitat-aware bead-process skill. The vocab agent independently flagged this exact wrapper as worth a habituate seed.

REFINED 2026-07-15: manifest-DRIVEN, manifest maintained at the habitat level. Prefer a DETERMINISTIC way to traverse + run the manifest; if full determinism isn't possible, a THIN skill that reads the manifest and executes the beads in order. (Supersedes the 'record deps on beads' idea — see habituate-23.2.)

title: LODESTONE: keep member repos agnostic (no habitat/cross-repo leakage)
content: LODESTONE (Ryan, 2026-07-15): a member repo must not know it belongs to habituate or any habitat, and we avoid cross-referencing other repos in artifacts we generate inside a member (code, seeds, beads). Habitat + cross-repo knowledge lives at the HABITAT level, never in members. Enforcement = a LANE LINE, not a lane divider: default to not crossing; NOT a hard rule to adhere to blindly. If there's a real reason to cross, CALL IT OUT and check with Ryan first. Context: my instinct twice defaulted to cross-repo/leaky solutions (member CLAUDE.local.md wiring; cross-repo bead deps) — this corrects that default. Promoted into habituate/CLAUDE.md + HABITAT.md so it actually loads. Related: habituate-16, habituate-23.2.
resolution: Recorded as a trellis in `CLAUDE.md` on 2026-07-16: Keep member repos agnostic: no habitat identity or cross-repo references in a member's artifacts (lane line; flag + check with Ryan before crossing)

title: Friction & streamlining from creating the jigsaw + codesets habitats
content: Investigated the jigsaw + codesets habitat-creation sessions (2026-07-16). codesets went CLEAN end-to-end (target workflow: precise members -> read member docs -> scaffold -> author -> doctor green -> commit -> clean tree). jigsaw snagged repeatedly (fuzzy membership + a mid-session interruption). Children capture the specific friction points + streamlining opportunities.

title: habituate is not on PATH in agent shells — the documented bare command fails
content:
HABITAT.md + habitat CLAUDE.mds tell agents to run bare 'habituate activity/create/doctor', but which habituate -> not found (home-manager wires it only for titan INTERACTIVE via a zsh function; the non-interactive Bash tool never loads it). Both create sessions pre-emptively worked around it (cd habituate && bin/habituate, or the absolute bin path); a jigsaw usage session hit the failure live. Fix: docs prescribe the bin/habituate form, or make bin/ resolvable for agents. Highest-value fix. Both habitats.

ADDRESSED 2026-07-16: symlinked habituate into ~/.local/bin (on PATH for interactive shells AND the non-interactive Bash tool); which habituate now resolves and bare 'habituate' works for agents. NB the symlink is manual, not declarative — Hammond could formalize it in home-manager for reproducibility.

title: habituate activity over-reports because of beads tooling artifacts
content:
.beads/export-state.json is untracked and not gitignored, so it dirties every member repo — AND because it lives under .beads/ the activity command counts it BOTH as dirty and as an uncommitted tasks/seeds change (double). A repo with zero real work shows dirty=1 tasks/seeds=1, reading as active. In jigsaw every dirty member was just tooling/build/config, nothing mid-flight. Fix: exclude known-tooling paths (esp .beads/export-state.json) from activity counts; and/or gitignore it upstream in beads setup. Both (jigsaw now, codesets by design later).

ADDRESSED 2026-07-16: activity now filters .beads/export-state.json (ACTIVITY_IGNORE) out of the dirty + tasks/seeds counts; verified a repo whose only change is that file reads dirty=0. Edge case: a brand-new untracked .beads/ dir collapses to '?? .beads/' in git porcelain and isn't filtered (rare; real repos have tracked .beads/).

title: Add a close-out guardrail: author -> commit the habitat CLAUDE.md
content:
codesets committed its authored CLAUDE.md (clean); jigsaw left it uncommitted (interrupted mid-handoff, M CLAUDE.md). No guardrail enforces the finish state. Options: create prints an explicit 'author then commit in the habitat repo' final checklist; a 'habituate seal/finish <name>' that commits; or doctor warns when a habitat CLAUDE.md is uncommitted or still contains TODO. jigsaw vs codesets.

ADDRESSED 2026-07-16: create commits the skeleton immediately and prints an explicit (1) author (2) commit-again close-out; doctor WARNs when a habitat CLAUDE.md still has TODO or is uncommitted.

title: Membership discovery is all manual investigation
content:
jigsaw's fuzzy ask (some members under main/, others under main/gems/, dormant candidates, and 'others I forgot') forced heavy manual discovery + an AskUserQuestion; codesets (2 exact repo names) needed none. Fix: create could accept a PARENT dir and suggest its git-repo children, flagging nested (gems/*) and dormant (stale last-commit / not referenced in any Gemfile) candidates for confirmation. jigsaw heavy vs codesets zero.

Promoted to bead habituate-n2a (2026-07-17).

title: Author-via-specialists can bake in plausible-but-wrong facts
content: A member-repo specialist asserted a wrong fact about that member's internals; it got written into the habitat map; Ryan corrected it from direct knowledge. Load-bearing claims from specialist subagents need a human accuracy pass. jigsaw only (codesets authored from repo docs, no specialist, no such error).
resolution: Mitigation decision: on any load-bearing fact, (a) when specialists disagree, adjudicate with a neutral, source-grounded resolver subagent that reads the code, and (b) keep a human accuracy pass on load-bearing claims before they enter a habitat doc. Instance this session: a member-internals adjudication; downstream doc-gap cleanup filed in the member's own tracker.

title: Crisp heuristic for habitat-doc depth vs a member's own docs
content: The 'briefing, not a wiki' boundary is hard to judge: while fixing an internal-mechanics fact the agent oscillated ~8 edits on whether a member's internal mechanics belong in the habitat doc or in the member's own docs. Need a concrete test for what lives at habitat level vs in a member's docs. jigsaw.
resolution: Answered by habituate-26 (the altitude test): a fact belongs in the habitat CLAUDE.md only if it lives at the seam between members (handoffs, routing, cross-repo misconceptions); a member-internal fact that keeps pulling into the briefing signals the member's own docs are thin — fix them there, keep the habitat to the seam.

title: Helper to surface each member's bead/seed prefix + tracker state
content:
Writing accurate routing forced agents to reverse-engineer each member's tracker prefix by grepping .beads/.seeds (codesets found csc- and int-; jigsaw had to hedge on members with no .beads/). Fix: a habituate helper (or doctor line) that prints each member's bead/seed prefix and whether .beads/ exists. Both.

ADDRESSED 2026-07-17: 'habituate members' surfaces each member's bead/seed prefix deterministically, and create auto-fills them into the CLAUDE.md map. See habituate-28.

title: Point the CLAUDE.md skeleton at a real exemplar, not bare TODOs
content:
Both sessions ignored the TODO skeleton and templated off a MATURE exemplar — the vocabulary habitat CLAUDE.md (codesets also read jigsaw's). Suggests the skeleton/template should POINT to an exemplar to copy the shape from, rather than only shipping bare TODOs. Both.

Promoted to bead habituate-z45 (2026-07-17).

Deferred 2026-07-17 (Ryan): the skeleton was reworked into templates/CLAUDE.md.tmpl (5a74cec, born after all three habitats) and has never been used to create one, so 25.8's evidence (sessions bypassing the OLD inline skeleton) does not apply to it. Re-evaluate ONLY after the first habitat is created with the new template, and only if the agent still bypasses it. Promoted bead habituate-z45 abandoned as premature.

title: Altitude test for authoring a habitat CLAUDE.md: keep only cross-repo facts (seams/handoffs, routing, shared misconceptions). A member-internal fact that keeps pulling into the briefing is a signal the member's own docs are thin — fix them there and keep the habitat to the seam (a copy becomes a second source of truth that silently drifts). Surfaced authoring the jigsaw habitat: a member's internal flow kept wanting into the briefing, and even that member's specialist misread it.
Deterministic aggregate query across members (bd ready / seeds ready), not ad-hoc for-loops
content:
An agent in a habitat dir, asked 'what's ready?', tends to run bd ready in the habitat (no beads -> nothing) instead of iterating the members — the confusion the vocab agent hit. HABITAT.md says tasks live in member beads but never says 'to survey all work, loop the members.' Per Ryan's determinism preference the fix is a deterministic aggregate command (e.g. 'habituate ready [<habitat>]' runs bd ready / seeds ready in each member and consolidates) rather than agents writing ad-hoc for-loops. Relates to habituate-23 (bead coordination) and habituate-25.7 (prefix helper).

BUILT 2026-07-17 as 'habituate ready [<habitat>]'; verified on vocabulary. Iterates members, runs bd ready + seeds ready in each, consolidates.

title: Member roster + prefixes live in the habitat CLAUDE.md; 'members' command is the source
content: Resolved the CLAUDE.md-vs-command tension (Ryan, 2026-07-17): the member roster + roles + bead/seed prefixes are stable, low-token, high-value, so they live IN the habitat CLAUDE.md map — just known, zero tool calls at use time. The deterministic 'habituate members' command is the SOURCE that authors/refreshes/verifies that section (its role is authoring/verification + a future doctor drift-check, NOT constant runtime querying). create now auto-fills each member's bead/seed prefix into the map skeleton (role still hand-authored). Static-known in the doc; command keeps it honest.

title: Architecture: thin leaves now, thick accreting leaves later; groups overlap
content: Leaves = each member repo's own local knowledge (its own docs/CLAUDE.md); habitats never centralize that. The habitat layer is a THIN shared overlay (symlinks + one CLAUDE.md of rules) — a view, not a copy. Groups OVERLAP: a repo can belong to several habitats (e.g. one repo in both a vocabulary and another habitat) — overlapping tags/views, not a partition. Thin now (authored rules); the personal domain later uses THICK leaves that accrete facts/events over time — same skeleton, different leaf thickness.

title: Scope: cross-host, cross-domain; professional vs personal, kept separate
content: One endeavor spanning two hosts and two domains, separate on purpose. Professional (here, on titan): thin RULE-habitats over our work repos — what we're building now. Personal/private (elsewhere): thick ACCRETING-knowledge habitats over life-areas — different host, different leaf thickness, later. Records stay separate — no bleed. Anything crossing is a HAND-CURATED slice, never a raw sync. The habituate README was itself such a curated professional slice. Prior professional research lives in finding-memo.

title: Design principles (hard constraints) for habitats
content: (1) LEGIBILITY ABOVE ALL — small, boring, inspectable; no daemon, database, build step, or synthesis engine; if you can't read it at a glance it's wrong. (2) AUTHORED, NOT SYNTHESIZED — rules are facts we write once, not distilled from documents; deliberately NO ingest/lint/re-indexing/invalidation machinery. (3) MINIMIZE INVENTED CONVENTIONS — real directories, symlinks, a real CLAUDE.md, standard markdown links; nothing only-our-tooling can follow. (4) PRIVACY BY PLACEMENT — institutional knowledge lives in the private habitat layer and must NEVER be committed into member repos that may be public.

title: Anti-patterns already paid for (two past internal tools)
content: A past project-grouping tool: don't couple 'where are my projects' to a stateful server, and don't let a grouping grow orchestration limbs (PR-triage, Dependabot, a database backend) — it rotted, mostly by becoming illegible. A past agent-knowledge tool: agent knowledge is NOT a built website or a frozen serialized dump divorced from the projects it describes — keep it in-repo / in a live, plain file. These are the concrete failures the design principles react to.

title: Naming: 'habituate' creates 'habitats'; astronomy reserved for another project
content: The concept is a 'habitat' (a group of related repos). The project/tool that creates habitats is 'habituate'. Astronomy words (constellation, etc.) are reserved for another project, so avoided — the earlier name 'constellation' is the same concept, renamed. Decided 2026-07-15: project dir renamed habitat/ -> habituate/, and materialized habitats live under habituate/habitats/ (gitignored).

title: First habitat built: vocabulary
content: First habitat (2026-07-15): 'vocabulary'. Members: ohdsi_supplemental_vocabs + vocabulation (core), one_offs/icd10cm (ICD-10 parser/producer), code_set_catalog + code_collector (read-only consumers). Materialized at habituate/habitats/vocabulary/ as symlinks + an authored ~1-page CLAUDE.md distilled from each member repo's docs and verified by the vocab specialist; kept as its OWN git repo. Goal: stop re-explaining vocab institutional knowledge — esp. that our build tooling auto-reintroduces our customizations onto pinned upstream releases, and the supplemental upstream does not churn as agents assume.

title: Wiring: member repos load a habitat via local CLAUDE.local.md import
content: REVISED 2026-07-15 (see habituate-16): per the habitat-agnostic-members vision, the per-member CLAUDE.local.md wiring is being DROPPED. Members stay agnostic; a habitat loads via the habitat dir being cwd (its own CLAUDE.md) + the symlinked habitats/CLAUDE.md parent — NOT via member imports. [Original design: each member got a local CLAUDE.local.md @import to the habitat CLAUDE.md, hidden via .git/info/exclude.]

title: Explicitly deferred directions (post first build)
content: NOT part of the first build: (a) a COMPOUNDING/SYNTHESIZING knowledge base — growing entity pages, ingest/query/lint over sources — aimed mainly at the personal domain's thick accreting leaves. (b) CROSS-HABITAT grouped queries + auto-routing of new knowledge to the right repo. (c) HUMAN-BROWSABLE rendering (MkDocs/Obsidian over the workspace) — optional; markdown is already readable. Keep the first build to a single habitat.
